Question: Write each of the following rational numbers as either a terminating or repeating decimal. ⓐ $$\frac{68}{17}$$ ⓑ $$\frac{8}{13}$$ ⓒ $$- \frac{17}{20}$$
o3 Answer:
To decide whether a fraction gives a terminating or a repeating decimal, do the division (or reduce the fraction first and examine its prime factors).

A terminating decimal occurs exactly when, after reducing the fraction to lowest terms, the denominator has no prime factors other than 2 or 5.  
Any other prime factor in the (reduced) denominator produces a repeating block.

a)  68/17  
First reduce, if possible: 68 ÷ 17 = 4.  
4 as a decimal is 4.0 (terminating).

b)  8/13  
The fraction is already in lowest terms and the denominator 13 contains a prime other than 2 or 5, so the decimal must repeat.  
Performing the division:

  0.615384 615384 …  

The six‐digit block 615384 repeats forever, so  
8/13 = 0.\overline{615384} (repeating).

c)  −17/20  
The fraction is already reduced. 20 = 2²·5, containing only 2’s and 5’s, so the decimal terminates.

 17 ÷ 20 = 0.85, hence  
−17/20 = −0.85 (terminating).

• A real number is rational if it can be written as a ratio of two integers.  
  Its decimal form either terminates or eventually repeats.  
• A real number that is not rational is irrational; its decimal form is non-terminating and non-repeating.

a) 7/77  
   7/77 = 1/11 = 0.090909… = 0.\overline{09}  
   Rational — repeating decimal.

b) √81  
   81 is a perfect square, so √81 = 9.  
   9 = 9.0 (terminating)  
   Rational — terminating decimal.

c) 4.27027002700027 …  
   The block “27” keeps reappearing but with an ever-increasing number of zeros between them, so no fixed block repeats.  
   Non-terminating and non-repeating ⇒ Irrational.

d) 91/13  
   91 ÷ 13 = 7 exactly.  
   7 = 7.0 (terminating)  
   Rational — terminating decimal.

e) √39  
   39 is not a perfect square, so √39 cannot be expressed as a ratio of integers.  
   Its decimal expansion is non-terminating and non-repeating.  
   Irrational.

Summary  
a) rational, repeating b) rational, terminating c) irrational d) rational, terminating e) irrational
